title: Trick Or Treat

jokes.text: It seems silly to put a napkin on an old pair of pants.
jokes.setup: Larry mentions napkin etiquette is very important
jokes.punchline: His dismissive comment about his own clothing contradicts the etiquette concern
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry self-aware observation about his own contradictions - caring about napkin etiquette while wearing old pants

jokes.text: Cobb salad ordering sequence with extensive substitutions
jokes.setup: Larry orders a Cobb salad
jokes.punchline: Removes bacon, eggs, essentially ordering a basic salad while insisting it's still a Cobb salad
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry character moment - his need to control everything, even menu items, while being completely unreasonable about it

jokes.text: I'm getting a David salad. Yeah, it is a David salad.
jokes.setup: Others question if he really wants a Cobb salad with all the substitutions
jokes.punchline: Larry acknowledges it's really a 'David salad' - named after himself
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta moment where Larry acknowledges his own absurdity while doubling down on it. The economy and self-awareness make this exceptional.

jokes.text: And I'll get agitation, if you can.
jokes.setup: Larry continues his detailed salad order
jokes.punchline: Orders 'agitation' as if it's a menu item
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment - Larry ordering his own emotional state as a side dish. The wordplay is unexpected and perfectly delivered.

jokes.text: I'm the 'Cobb' of Cobb salad.
jokes.setup: Cliff reveals his grandfather created the Cobb salad
jokes.punchline: Direct statement claiming ownership of the famous salad
jokes.explanation: Sets up the episode's main deception plot. Clean delivery but fairly straightforward claim.

jokes.text: This is a nice little bullshit story.
jokes.setup: Cliff tells elaborate story about grandfather inventing Cobb salad
jokes.punchline: Larry's blunt dismissal of the story
jokes.explanation: Larry's characteristic bluntness cutting through social niceties. Rewatch bonus as we later learn Larry was actually right.

jokes.text: You know, my grandfather's name was Harold Bingo. He invented Bingo. Are you aware of that?
jokes.setup: Larry mocks Cliff's claim about inventing Cobb salad
jokes.punchline: Makes up equally ridiculous claim about his grandfather inventing Bingo
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry response - using absurdity to point out the absurdity of Cliff's claim. The name 'Harold Bingo' is perfectly ridiculous.

jokes.text: We should have sex sometime. - Yes, we should.
jokes.setup: Larry throws out inappropriate comment during others' conversation
jokes.punchline: Shelley agrees, creating awkward moment
jokes.explanation: Classic Curb cringe moment that sets up later plot complications. The inappropriate timing and unexpected agreement create discomfort.

jokes.text: Are you Jewish?
jokes.setup: Walter approaches Larry who's whistling
jokes.punchline: Blunt, inappropriate question out of nowhere
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for the Wagner confrontation. The bluntness and inappropriateness are shocking and funny.

jokes.text: You wanna check my penis? Is that what you wanna do?
jokes.setup: Walter asks if Larry is Jewish
jokes.punchline: Larry escalates to absurd physical verification
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry escalation - taking the question to its logical extreme in the most inappropriate way possible.

jokes.text: You're a self-loathing Jew. - Am I? - Yes! I do hate myself, but it has nothing to do with being Jewish.
jokes.setup: Walter accuses Larry of being a self-loathing Jew
jokes.punchline: Larry admits to self-hatred but clarifies it's not because he's Jewish
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Larry moment - admitting to self-hatred while completely missing the point. Perfect character work and unexpected response.

jokes.text: Where's your Judaism? Judaism? Where are you? Where are you, Judaism?
jokes.setup: Walter's increasing agitation with Larry
jokes.punchline: Calling out to Judaism like it's a lost pet
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation of Walter's outrage - personifying Judaism as something that can be called to like a missing pet.

jokes.text: They should put a muzzle on you. You're foaming at the mouth.
jokes.setup: Walter's increasingly unhinged behavior
jokes.punchline: Larry's calm observation about Walter's state
jokes.explanation: Larry's typical understated response to chaos - treating extreme behavior with casual observation.

jokes.text: Can you keep it down? Hey, I'm allowed to eat popcorn.
jokes.setup: Someone asks Larry to be quieter in the theater
jokes.punchline: Larry's defensive response about his popcorn rights
jokes.explanation: Standard Larry behavior - defending his right to do something annoying while missing the social cue.

jokes.text: Is it smoke? What comes out of your mouth in cold water? - It's breath. - But looks like smoke.
jokes.setup: Discussing the movie scene with cold water
jokes.punchline: Larry's confusion about basic physics of breath in cold
jokes.explanation: Minor character moment showing Larry's tendency to fixate on irrelevant details.

jokes.text: Tomorrow, I'm sitting around. I'm not doing anything. Sitting around?
jokes.setup: Larry lies about not having plans to spare Cliff's feelings
jokes.punchline: Gets caught in the lie and has to double down awkwardly
jokes.explanation: Sets up the golf deception plot. Larry's awkward repetition shows his discomfort with the lie.

jokes.text: I wasn't bored. I was yawning because... Can't somebody be tired and yawn? I mean it's a biological function.
jokes.setup: Cheryl criticizes Larry for yawning during movie
jokes.punchline: Larry's over-explanation and appeal to biology
jokes.explanation: Typical Larry defense - using technical correctness to avoid social responsibility.

jokes.text: If I was handicapped, I'd want people to tell me that they're sitting around.
jokes.setup: Defending his decision to lie to Cliff about his plans
jokes.punchline: Larry's misguided logic about what disabled people want to hear
jokes.explanation: Larry's misguided attempt at sensitivity backfires through his complete lack of understanding.

jokes.text: Thank you. I don't tuck in that much. Golf makes me tuck in. - You look good tucked in. - I'm rarely tucked in.
jokes.setup: Assistant compliments Larry's golf outfit
jokes.punchline: Larry's over-analysis of his tucking-in habits
jokes.explanation: Sets up the golf outfit recurring gag. Larry's overthinking of simple clothing choices is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Trick or treat. - Happy Halloween. - Festive?
jokes.setup: Kids trick-or-treating at Larry's door
jokes.punchline: Larry's sarcastic one-word summary of Halloween spirit
jokes.explanation: Larry's disdain for forced celebration captured in one word. Perfect economy.

jokes.text: Oh, my God. Shelley, no. That was... No, that was just a joke because they were talking so much.
jokes.setup: Shelley apparently took Larry's proposition seriously
jokes.punchline: Larry's panicked backtracking on the phone
jokes.explanation: Payoff to earlier setup. Larry's panic and over-explanation make the situation worse.

jokes.text: My friend Cliff Cobb lied to me about the Cobb salad! His grandfather didn't invent it. Bob Cobb did!
jokes.setup: Larry discovers the truth about Cobb salad origin
jokes.punchline: His outrage at being lied to about salad history
jokes.explanation: Great payoff showing Larry was right all along. His indignation about salad history is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: I don't think so. - You kidding? - It's Halloween. Yeah, I know. Doesn't mean you're entitled to go around to people's homes and bilk them out of candy.
jokes.setup: Teenagers trick-or-treating without costumes
jokes.punchline: Larry's use of 'bilk' and treating candy-giving as fraud
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry response - treating Halloween as a serious contractual matter. The word 'bilk' is unexpectedly formal and funny.

jokes.text: Where's your costumes? - I'm being my sister. - I'm a teacher. - Wow, I would like to get my seat in your class.
jokes.setup: Girls give weak costume explanations
jokes.punchline: Larry's inappropriately flirtatious response
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable cringe moment that adds to the escalating conflict. Not Larry's strongest moment.

jokes.text: Asshole! Fuck you!
jokes.setup: Larry refuses to give candy to costume-less teens
jokes.punchline: Their immediate aggressive response
jokes.explanation: Predictable escalation but sets up the house vandalism plot. The shock value works in context.

jokes.text: Doesn't mean you're entitled to go around to people's homes and bilk them out of candy.
jokes.setup: Girls expect candy on Halloween
jokes.punchline: Larry frames trick-or-treating as a criminal enterprise
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry - treating normal social interaction as serious crime. 'Bilk' is a perfect word choice.

jokes.text: I didn't know that it was gonna be 'felony or treat.'
jokes.setup: Discussing the vandalism aftermath
jokes.punchline: Play on 'trick or treat' replacing 'trick' with 'felony'
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay that perfectly captures Larry's escalation from minor social rules to criminal behavior.

jokes.text: Trick-or-treating at 40? Free candy, I'm 40 years old, I want free candy!
jokes.setup: Arguing about age limits for trick-or-treating
jokes.punchline: Larry taking the logic to its absurd conclusion
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Larry's ability to take any argument to extremes. The enthusiasm is funny.

jokes.text: It was sort of Elvira-ish. She looked like Elvira. - So she was kind of in a little bit of costume there. The Elvira thing. - No, that wasn't a costume.
jokes.setup: Larry describes one of the vandals as Elvira-like
jokes.punchline: Officer thinks this counts as a costume; Larry insists it's just description
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Larry's preciseness about language creating confusion.

jokes.text: That's a hate crime. We're a set, we're a group. You can't call us bald assholes.
jokes.setup: Larry explains the 'bald asshole' graffiti
jokes.punchline: Claims bald people are a protected class
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Larry logic - treating his baldness as grounds for hate crime protection. The earnestness makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Sir, I'm bald, I'm not offended. - With all due respect, Officer Byrd, you are not bald. You have chosen to shave your hair.
jokes.setup: Officer claims not to be offended as a bald person
jokes.punchline: Larry's distinction between natural baldness and fashion choice
jokes.explanation: Excellent Larry moment - his need to categorize everything precisely, even baldness. The 'with all due respect' formality is perfect.

jokes.text: we don't consider you part of the bald community. With all due respect.
jokes.setup: Continuing explanation about real vs. chosen baldness
jokes.punchline: Formal exclusion from the 'bald community'
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the baldness hierarchy. The repeated 'with all due respect' adds formality to absurdity.

jokes.text: It's kind of a social contract you enter when you open that door.
jokes.setup: Discussing Halloween expectations
jokes.punchline: Treating trick-or-treat as a formal social contract
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response that validates Larry's formalistic thinking about social customs.

jokes.text: I will not be intimidated. Even on Halloween.
jokes.setup: Officer suggests giving candy to avoid problems
jokes.punchline: Larry frames it as a matter of principle against intimidation
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry - turning a minor social situation into a matter of personal integrity and courage.

jokes.text: How old is she, 5 or 6 years old?
jokes.setup: Larry says he can't play golf on wife's birthday
jokes.punchline: Donald questions why Larry must spend entire day with wife
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of male friendship logic. Treating wife like a child is crude but funny.

jokes.text: You're sick. - You can wear clothes, these clothes don't... - Stop it.
jokes.setup: Donald catches Larry in golf clothes again
jokes.punchline: Donald's frustrated dismissal of Larry's explanations
jokes.explanation: Continues the golf clothing deception plot. Donald's exasperation is earned.

jokes.text: 'I don't play golf.' Look at that shirt.
jokes.setup: Larry wearing obvious golf attire while denying he plays
jokes.punchline: Donald calls out the obvious contradiction
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the running golf theme. Simple but effective contradiction comedy.

jokes.text: I'm talking about the little knee game with Shelley. I'm talking about the arm over the neck. You're busted.
jokes.setup: Cliff confronts Larry about inappropriate behavior
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was watching Larry's subtle advances
jokes.explanation: Good revelation that Cliff saw everything. 'Knee game' is a funny phrase.

jokes.text: Let me ask his wife to screw me. That's how you joke around?
jokes.setup: Larry tries to explain his earlier proposition as a joke
jokes.punchline: Cliff's interpretation of Larry's 'joking around'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the confrontation. Cliff's phrasing makes Larry's behavior sound even worse.

jokes.text: my grandfather invented the Cobb salad! - That's me who does that?
jokes.setup: Cliff accuses Larry of lying about grandfather inventions
jokes.punchline: Larry's sarcastic response, given that Cliff is the actual liar
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic moment where Larry is accused of exactly what Cliff actually did. The dramatic irony is excellent.

jokes.text: The Cobb salad, my friend, was invented in 1937 by Bob Cobb at the Brown Derby!
jokes.setup: Cliff continues to insist his grandfather invented Cobb salad
jokes.punchline: Larry delivers the factual correction with authority
jokes.explanation: Satisfying payoff where Larry gets to be right with facts. His triumphant delivery is earned.

jokes.text: That son of a bitch stole that salad from my grandfather.
jokes.setup: Confronted with facts about Cobb salad invention
jokes.punchline: Cliff escalates the lie into a conspiracy theory
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - rather than admit the lie, Cliff creates an even bigger conspiracy. 'Salad theft' is absurd.

jokes.text: You're dead, you son of a bitch!
jokes.setup: Larry proves Cliff wrong about the Cobb salad
jokes.punchline: Cliff's extreme threat over salad history
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how ridiculous the stakes have become over salad history.

jokes.text: Hey, Elvira. - Some fucking weirdo is trying to talk to me.
jokes.setup: Larry approaches the girl he described as Elvira-like
jokes.punchline: She calls him a 'fucking weirdo' while on the phone
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Larry tracking down his vandals. The immediate dismissal is funny.

jokes.text: Larry David! What brings you to our Jewish home? - Well, well, now it all makes perfect sense.
jokes.setup: Walter answers the door and recognizes Larry
jokes.punchline: Larry realizes this explains the daughter's behavior
jokes.explanation: Great plot convergence moment. Larry's realization that the vandal is Walter's daughter ties storylines together nicely.

jokes.text: What a shanda Larry David is. Not only are you a lousy Jew, but you're a lousy human being!
jokes.setup: Walter's increasing outrage at Larry
jokes.punchline: Public denunciation calling Larry a 'shanda' (shame)
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Walter's campaign against Larry. The use of 'shanda' adds cultural specificity.

jokes.text: He screws the wife of a man in a wheelchair!
jokes.setup: Walter's public denunciation continues
jokes.punchline: Exaggerated accusation about Larry and Shelley
jokes.explanation: Walter's exaggeration turns a bad joke into an affair accusation. The wheelchair detail adds to the perceived awfulness.

jokes.text: Maybe you should have given me some candy!
jokes.setup: Walter's daughter was the Halloween vandal
jokes.punchline: Larry's final comeback connecting all the conflicts
jokes.explanation: Excellent episode-ending callback that connects the Halloween incident to the Wagner confrontation. Perfect Curb structure.

jokes.text: That woman didn't give me the bracelet, so I had to come up with something.
jokes.setup: Larry showing up with alternative birthday gift
jokes.punchline: Admits his gift is a desperate improvisation due to bracelet situation
jokes.explanation: Good callback to bracelet situation. Larry's honesty about his desperation is in character.

jokes.text: How about six?
jokes.setup: Larry refuses to play 18 or 9 holes because it's Cheryl's birthday
jokes.punchline: Donald's desperate negotiation down to 6 holes
jokes.explanation: Good ending beat showing Donald's persistent golf obsession. The negotiation down to minimal holes is funny.

tmdb_overview: Larry offends two uncostumed trick-or-treaters by not giving them candy, so they show him the "trick" side of Halloween. He also alienates Cheryl's screenwriter friend Cliff and a Jewish neighbor AND spoils his romantic anniversary gift when Jeff arrives to play a round of golf.
